name: Stromboli
recipeIngredient: Red or green pepper (1, thinly sliced)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F.
2. Line a 10x15 baking pan with parchment paper or trimmed clean brown paper.
3. In a nonstick skillet, heat olive oil over medium-high heat.
4. Add onion, mushrooms, walnuts, thyme, and cracked pepper; cook until vegetables soften, about 8 minutes.
5. Add white wine; cook until most of the liquid evaporates, about 3 minutes.
6. Remove from heat; stir in scallions. Transfer to a bowl.
7. In the same skillet, heat remaining oil over high heat.
8. Add red or green pepper strips; cook until just softened, about 6 minutes. Remove and set aside.
9. Roll pizza dough into a 13x16 rectangle on a lightly floured surface.
10. Transfer to the baking pan. Cut out two 2-inch squares at each corner and reserve scraps for decoration if desired.
11. Combine egg yolk with 2 tablespoons water.
12. Spread mushroom mixture in the center of the dough, about 4 inches wide, leaving notched ends empty.
13. Top mushroom mixture with ham, then cheese, zucchini mixture, and pepper strips.
14. Brush some egg yolk around each cut corner. Fold short ends of dough over filling.
15. Using scissors, cut dough on either side of the filling into 1-inch-wide strips, cutting in from the outside edge to within 3/4 inch of the filling.
16. Brush bottom and top ends with some egg yolk. Crisscross strips over filling, pulling a little to bring 3/4 inch of uncut dough up around the filling.
17. Brush with egg yolk; sprinkle poppy seeds over and/or decorate with dough scraps if desired.
18. Let rise for 20 minutes in a warm place. Bake for 45 minutes or until crust is golden and filling sizzles. Cool for 10 minutes before slicing.
TIP: You can freeze the stromboli just before the dough rises for up to 3 weeks. Thaw in the refrigerator for 4 hours, let rise for 20 minutes in a warm place before baking.
